▶ #102 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ stop-long-phrase 1143 tokens · 122.1 tok/s

API variant: stop-long-phrase temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096 stop: In conclusion
Perf finish: stop load: 2.0s gen: 9.36s prompt_tok: 41 comp_tok: 1143 tok/s: 122.1
🎯 Test Intent
Tests stop on a long multi-word phrase. Feature: stop with multi-token string.
Expected: essay contains paragraphs 1-2 about renewable energy, stops before
"In conclusion" appears. Tests buffer-based stop detection across token boundaries.
PROMPT Write a 3-paragraph essay about renewable energy. Start the last paragraph with "In conclusion".

▶ #78 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ stop-double-newline 742 tokens · 99.9 tok/s

API variant: stop-double-newline temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096 stop:
Perf finish: stop load: 1.0s gen: 7.43s prompt_tok: 37 comp_tok: 742 tok/s: 99.9
🎯 Test Intent
Tests stop on double newline. Feature: stop parameter with "\n\n".
Expected: exactly one paragraph (about the ocean). The second paragraph about
mountains should NOT appear. finish_reason="stop".
PROMPT Write a short paragraph about the ocean. Then write a second paragraph about mountains.

▶ #82 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ stop-period 741 tokens · 98.0 tok/s

API variant: stop-period temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096 stop: .
Perf finish: stop load: 1.0s gen: 7.56s prompt_tok: 30 comp_tok: 741 tok/s: 98.0

Tests stop on punctuation (period). Feature: stop parameter with ".".
Expected: only the beginning of the first sentence, cut off before the first period.
finish_reason="stop". Very aggressive stop — output may be just a few words.
PROMPT Tell me about the sun in three sentences.

▶ #107 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ stop-immediate 817 tokens · 96.7 tok/s

API variant: stop-immediate baseline: yes temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096 stop: The, I, A
Perf finish: stop load: 1.0s gen: 8.45s prompt_tok: 38 comp_tok: 817 tok/s: 96.7
🎯 Test Intent
Tests immediate stop (first token matches). Feature: stop on common first tokens.
Expected: empty or near-empty output. The model's first token is likely "The", "I",
or "A" — all are in the stop list. finish_reason="stop", completion_tokens near 0.
PROMPT Name three primary colors and explain why they matter in design. Answer concisely.

▶ #109 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ stop-special-chars 824 tokens · 96.5 tok/s

API variant: stop-special-chars baseline: yes temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096 stop: **

🎯 Test Intent
Tests stop on markdown special characters. Feature: stop with "**".
Expected: output stops at first "**" bold marker. Only partial text before the first
bold-formatted word. Tests that special regex characters in stop are handled correctly.
PROMPT Name three primary colors and explain why they matter in design. Answer concisely.

▶ #158 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ tool-call-xml 762 tokens · 96.2 tok/s

CLI afm: --tool-call-parser xmlFunction
API variant: tool-call-xml temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096
Perf finish: stop load: 1.0s gen: 7.93s prompt_tok: 31 comp_tok: 762 tok/s: 96.2
🎯 Test Intent
Tests explicit xmlFunction parser. Feature: --tool-call-parser xmlFunction.
Expected: get_weather tool call with city="Paris", unit="celsius".
Same as tool-call-auto but explicitly sets the parser — verifies the flag works.
PROMPT What is the weather in Paris in celsius?

▶ #30 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ eli5 1270 tokens · 95.9 tok/s

CLI system: Explain everything as if the user is 5 years old. Use simple words only.
API variant: eli5 temp: 0.7 max_tokens: 4096
Perf finish: stop load: 1.0s gen: 13.24s prompt_tok: 39 comp_tok: 1270 tok/s: 95.9
🎯 Test Intent
Tests system prompt persona (ELI5). Feature: system message handling.
Expected: very simple words, analogies a child would understand, no jargon.
If output uses words like "superposition" or "entanglement", persona failed.
PROMPT Tell me about quantum computing.

▶ #2 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ greedy 546 tokens · 95.9 tok/s

API variant: greedy temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096
Perf finish: stop load: 11.0s gen: 5.69s prompt_tok: 35 comp_tok: 546 tok/s: 95.9
🎯 Test Intent
Tests greedy decoding (temperature=0). Feature: deterministic sampling.
Expected: factually correct, structured 3-bullet response about Rayleigh scattering.
Compare with default/high-temp — this should be the least creative but most precise.
PROMPT Explain why the sky is blue in exactly 3 bullet points.

▶ #161 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ tool-call-multi 1217 tokens · 95.9 tok/s

API variant: tool-call-multi temp: 0.0 max_tokens: 4096
Perf finish: stop load: 1.0s gen: 12.69s prompt_tok: 35 comp_tok: 1217 tok/s: 95.9
🎯 Test Intent
Tests multiple tool calls in one response. Feature: parallel tool calling.
Expected: TWO tool calls — get_weather(city="London") AND get_time(timezone="Asia/Tokyo").
finish_reason="tool_calls". Tests that the parser handles multiple <tool_call> blocks.
PROMPT What is the weather in London and what time is it in Tokyo?

still accurately describe quantum computing concepts.

▶ #170 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ tool-call-nullable-param 587 tokens · 95.6 tok/s

Tests tool call with nullable parameter schema (regression: PR #33).
Expected: get_weather tool call with location as a string. The "units" parameter has
anyOf [string, null] with default null — this must NOT crash Jinja template rendering.
finish_reason="tool_calls". Validates nullable schemas don't cause server errors.
PROMPT What is the weather in Chicago?

▶ #130 mlx-community/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit @ response-format-json 1305 tokens · 95.6 tok/s

"Python", 1991, "Guido van Rossum". Verify is_valid_json=true. Uses prompt injection
(not constrained decoding) — output may include markdown fences around JSON.
PROMPT Return a JSON object with keys "language", "year_created", and "creator" for Python.

Tests KV cache size limit. Feature: --max-kv-size (limits context window).
Expected: coherent ML summary. With 2048 max KV, very long prompts would fail
but this prompt is short enough to fit. Verify no truncation or crash.
PROMPT Summarize the key ideas of machine learning in a few paragraphs.

No numbering, no punctuation, no extra text. Any deviation = instruction following failure.
PROMPT Output EXACTLY 3 lines. Each line must contain exactly one word. The words must be: apple, banana, cherry. No other text, no numbering, no punctuation.

Expected: same output quality as prefill-default. TTFT may differ — larger steps
process more tokens per GPU pass (faster prefill but more memory per pass).

Tests stop inside json_object response format. Feature: stop + response_format: json_object.
Expected: JSON output stops when "age" key name appears. Output is truncated JSON.
Unlike guided-json, json_object uses prompt injection — stop still applies to raw output.
PROMPT Generate a JSON object with keys "name", "age", and "city" for a person named Carol.
